mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates below 0.5% reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of observations when FEDFUNDS < 0.5%, or rolling Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately suppress equity valuations over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03) during expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns in >50% of cases, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index growth above 0.5% month-over-month reflects expanding manufacturing capacity that translates into higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal predicts negative returns during early-cycle phases, or the 20-day return correlation with INDPRO growth drops below 0.05 in rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation print exceeding 0.3% triggers immediate market repricing of future monetary tightening, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day returns are positive in >65% of high-inflation trigger months, or the signal predictive accuracy vanishes when inflation remains below 2% annually.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent monthly nonfarm payroll additions above 200,000 sustain aggregate income growth and corporate revenue expansion, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year Rank IC falls below 0.02, or 60-day forward returns are negative in >55% of months with payroll growth exceeding 200,000.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth exceeding 2.0% combined with positive retail sales momentum confirms sustained consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests, or 20-day returns are negative in >50% of triggered quarters.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the industrial production index signals supply-side contraction that immediately weighs on equity valuations over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict negative 5-day returns in >60% of observations during late-cycle phases, or the rolling Rank IC remains below 0.03 over 3-year windows.
